metadata.title: The Non-Delegation Audit: Four Tests for Tasks That Should Never Go to Claude

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: The four tests plus the added fifth ground-truth test are what I want to run against my actual task list, since one borderline task flipped to DO NOT DELEGATE only once 'no external ground truth' was checked. I'll give you five tasks I'm considering handing to Claude. Please (1) route each through the four tests for sensitive data, irreversibility, human-only relationship context, and unverifiable output, (2) apply the fifth ground-truth test and give a verdict with a blocking reason per failure, and (3) tell me which category I'd have discovered the hard way.

props.command: The four tests plus the added fifth ground-truth test are what I want to run against my actual task list, since one borderline task flipped to DO NOT DELEGATE only once 'no external ground truth' was checked. I'll give you five tasks I'm considering handing to Claude. Please (1) route each through the four tests for sensitive data, irreversibility, human-only relationship context, and unverifiable output, (2) apply the fifth ground-truth test and give a verdict with a blocking reason per failure, and (3) tell me which category I'd have discovered the hard way.
props.segment: The Non-Delegation Audit: Four Tests for

beats.narration_text: The most dangerous AI delegation failures happen when you can delegate — but shouldn't.

Four categories of non-delegatable tasks: involves regulated or sensitive data such as HIPAA or legal filings; action is irreversible — sends emails, publishes content, modifies production; requires relationship context only the human has; output cannot be verified without doing the work yourself. Missing any of these is how you discover the category the hard way.

For each of these tasks, determine if delegation is appropriate. Apply four tests: involves regulated or sensitive data, action is irreversible, requires relationship context only the human has, output cannot be verified without doing the work yourself.

props.command: claude "For each of these tasks, determine if delegation is appropriate. Apply four tests: (1) involves regulated/sensitive data, (2) action is irreversible, (3) requires relationship context only the human has, (4) output cannot be verified without doing the work yourself. Return: task, verdict (delegate/do-not-delegate), blocking reason." < tasks.txt

beats.narration_text: The delegation audit script runs tasks through 4 tests and produces a verdict with a blocking reason for each.

Animated decision tree: 5 tasks routed through 4 tests. Two tasks route to DO NOT DELEGATE — one fails the regulated data test, one fails the irreversible action test. Three tasks route to DELEGATE after passing all four tests.
Add fifth test: is the output testable against an external ground truth?

props.command: claude "Re-run the audit with a fifth test: is the output testable against an external ground truth? Tasks that cannot be externally verified should not be delegated." < tasks.txt

beats.narration_text: Same tree with fifth test GROUND TRUTH added. One borderline task that previously routed to DELEGATE now fails the fifth test and routes to DO NOT DELEGATE. Blocking reason: no external ground truth to verify synthesis claims against.

The non-delegation classifier is not about distrust — it's about accountability. You can't hold Claude responsible for a legal filing or a medical recommendation. The four tests exist so you know which category you're in before you discover it through a failure.
Next: assemble a research packet with source traceability.
Nik Bear Brown. Brutalist and Educational AI. nikbearbrown.com.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.
